prediction.reasoning: The point estimate of $297.60 is derived by assuming a very slight upward drift (approximately 0.1% over 2 days) from the current price, reflecting a general positive long-term tendency in equity markets, though minimal for such a short horizon. The confidence intervals are calculated using an estimated historical daily volatility for JPM (or similar large-cap financial stocks, approximately 1.1% standard deviation of daily returns), scaled for a 2-day period (using the square root of time rule). These are then applied to the point estimate using standard Z-scores for each confidence level, assuming a normal distribution of returns. It is important to note that short-term stock price predictions are highly uncertain and subject to numerous unpredictable market factors.
